name: Jalapeno Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 6 ears corn or 3 cups frozen whole kernel corn
1 to 2 fresh jalapeno peppers, seeded and finely chopped
1/4 of a 7 1/2 ounce jar roasted red sweet peppers, drained -- and chopped (1/4 cup) or 1 jar diced pimento, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. If using fresh corn, cut off the kernels from the ears.
2. In a blender container, combine half of the corn and the chicken broth. Blend until nearly smooth.
3. In a large saucepan, combine the blended broth mixture and the remaining corn kernels.
4. Stir in the milk or light cream, finely chopped jalapeno peppers, and chopped red peppers or pimento (diced pimento).
5. Heat through the chowder until it is heated thoroughly.
6. Serve each portion topped with crumbled feta cheese and garnished with whole peppers if desired.
7. Makes 4 side dish servings.
